name: San Miguel de Aralar Figure-Eight Loop
name_original: Alsasua-Beruete-Lekunberri-Saldias-Irurtzun – gran 8 por las sierras de Aralar
description: This difficult 94.7-mile road cycling route through the Aralar mountains offers challenging climbs and scenic views in Navarra, Spain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Altsasu/Alsasua offers diverse landscapes in the Foral Community of Navarre, bordering Gipuzkoa. The region is characterized by challenging mountain passes such as Opakua, Lierni, Otzaurte, and Lizarraga, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Cyclists can navigate through the Burunda valley, surrounded by mountains like Alzania and the Sierra de Urbasa, and explore natural parks including Urbasa-Andía and Aralar. These areas feature extensive beech forests, meadows, and limestone formations, with well-paved surfaces on most passes suitable for road bikes. Best road cycling routes around Altsasu/Alsasua The most popular road cycling route is Alsasua-Beruete-Lekunberri-Saldias-Irurtzun – great 8 through the Aralar mountains, a 94.7 miles (152.4 km) trail that takes 8 hours 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the Aralar mountains, offering significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Opakua Pass – Urbasa Hairpin Bends loop from Altsasu/Alsasua-Pueblo, a difficult 43.8 miles (70.5 km) path. This route features the challenging Opakua Pass and the scenic Urbasa Hairpin Bends. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Lizarraga – Lizarraga Viewpoint loop from Altsasu-Alsasua, a 32.4 miles (52.1 km) trail leading through Puerto de Lizarraga, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Road cycling around Altsasu/Alsasua is defined by challenging mountain passes, diverse natural parks, and the scenic Burunda valley. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The routes in Altsasu/Alsasua are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Altsasu/Alsasua's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Altsasu/Alsasua?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Altsasu/Alsasua, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 5 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 12 challenging routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Altsasu/Alsasua?
A: Road cycling in Altsasu/Alsasua is characterized by diverse landscapes. You'll encounter challenging mountain passes like Opakua, Lierni, Otzaurte, and Lizarraga, as well as scenic rides through the Burunda valley. The region is surrounded by mountains like Alzania and the Sierra de Urbasa, and features natural parks such as Urbasa-Andía and Aralar, offering a mix of ascents and rolling terrain.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Altsasu/Alsasua offers several routes suitable for beginners. While many routes feature challenging mountain passes, there are 5 easy routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38246838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Lizarraga – Lizarraga Viewpoint loop from Altsasu-Alsasua&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that provides a rewarding experience without extreme difficulty.

Q: What are some of the most challenging road cycling routes around Altsasu/Alsasua?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984955728/alsasua-beruete-lekunberri-saldias-irurtzun-great-8-through-the-aralar-mountains" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alsasua-Beruete-Lekunberri-Saldias-Irurtzun – great 8 through the Aralar mountains&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 152 km route with significant elevation gain. Another tough option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1286996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Opakua Pass – Urbasa Hairpin Bends loop from Altsasu/Alsasua-Pueblo&lt;/a&gt;, known for its challenging climbs.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling in Altsasu/Alsasua?
A: The region boasts stunning natural beauty. You can cycle through the Urbasa-Andía Natural Park, known for its beech forests and limestone formations, or explore the higher elevation meadows of Aralar Natural Park. The area also features impressive viewpoints like Mirador Ikuspegia and Ubaba Viewpoint (Balcón de Pilatos), offering panoramic views after a climb.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area around Altsasu/Alsasua has historical and cultural significance. You might encounter megalithic monuments such as those at Muñaan, Balankaleku, and Saratxakotegi. Additionally, you can find various hermitages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3461034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3819208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Marina&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible via hiking routes that intersect with cycling areas.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Altsasu/Alsasua?
A: The road cycling routes in Altsasu/Alsasua are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the challenge of the mountain passes, and the beautiful natural parks like Urbasa-Andía and Aralar, which provide a fresh ambiance and stunning scenery.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Altsasu/Alsasua are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38246838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Lizarraga – Lizarraga Viewpoint loop from Altsasu-Alsasua&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39546341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lierni Pass – Otzaurte loop from Altsasu-Alsasua&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied distances and difficulties.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Altsasu/Alsasua?
A: The region offers a fresh ambiance with diverse shades of green in spring and summer, making these seasons ideal for cycling. Autumn also provides beautiful russet colors. While some mountain passes might be affected by snow in winter, many routes remain accessible, though conditions should be checked. The well-paved surfaces on most passes are suitable for road bikes throughout much of the year.

Q: Are there facilities for cyclists in the area, such as repair shops or accommodation?
A: Yes, the region is supportive of cyclists. The Urbasa campsite, located within the Urbasa-Andía Natural Park, features a cycle tourism center. This center offers amenities like repair workshops, cleaning areas, storage facilities, and information on local routes, making it a convenient stop for cyclists.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes on every route are not detailed, the presence of a cycle tourism center at Urbasa Campsite suggests that facilities catering to cyclists, including potential refreshment stops, are available in key areas. Altsasu/Alsasua itself and other towns along the routes will offer various options for food and drink.

Q: What are some notable mountain passes for road cycling in this region?
A: The Altsasu/Alsasua region is famous for its challenging mountain passes. Key climbs include Opakua Pass, Lierni Pass, Otzaurte, and Puerto de Lizarraga. The Urbasa Pass, leading into the Sierra de Urbasa, is another prominent cycling climb that offers both physical challenge and panoramic views.
tips: Located between the Aralar mountains, it is a renowned spiritual center and one of the oldest in Navarre. Its 12th century Romanesque architecture is an architectural jewel worth visiting and admiring.
A very picturesque place above 800 meters above sea level, ideal for the summer months when the forest provides its shade on the quiet road.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for the Alsasua-Beruete-Lekunberri-Saldias-Irurtzun cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a "difficult" road ride, demanding a very good level of fitness. With over 152 kilometers and approximately 2,670 meters of cumulative ascent, it's a challenging tour designed for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly on asphalt and paved roads, ensuring a smooth surface suitable for road bikes. You'll encounter demanding ascents, particularly on climbs like the Alto de Uitzi (802 m) and the Alto de Beruete. While the road conditions are generally good, be prepared for consistent gradients, with some sections reaching up to 7.8%.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning natural landscapes, a significant highlight is the San Miguel de Aralar Sanctuary, a 12th-century Romanesque architectural gem. You'll also traverse mountain passes like the Alto de Beruete, Alto de Orokieta, and Alto de Uitzi (802 m), offering picturesque views and challenging climbs.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Aralar mountains offer lush green scenery, and the Alto de Beruete provides welcome shade during warmer months. Generally, late spring, summer, and early autumn are ideal, offering pleasant temperatures for cycling. Always check the local weather forecast before you go, especially for mountain passes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the towns along the route, such as Alsasua, Lekunberri, or Irurtzun. These towns serve as good starting points, offering various amenities. Specific parking areas may vary, so it's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in Aralar Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As this route passes through the Aralar mountains, which are part of the Aralar Natural Park (a Site of Community Importance within the Natura 2000 network), there are generally no specific cycling permits or entrance fees required for public roads. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the protected natural environment.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Given that this is a long and challenging road cycling route, it is not suitable for dogs. The high speeds, distances, and road surfaces are not appropriate for canine companions.

faqs.question: Is the 'great 8 through the Aralar mountains' route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Due to its significant length (152 km), considerable elevation gain (2,670 m), and classification as a "difficult" ride requiring "very good fitness," this route is not recommended for families or beginner cyclists. It's best suited for experienced road cyclists looking for a demanding challenge.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or rest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns like Alsasua, Lekunberri, and Irurtzun, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities for rest and refueling. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially between towns, as services may be sparse on mountain sections.

faqs.question: What are the most challenging climbs on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features several demanding ascents. Key climbs include the Alto de Uitzi (802 m), known for its steady gradient through forested areas, and the Alto de Beruete. These climbs require sustained effort, with some sections reaching gradients of up to 7.8%.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling through the Aralar mountains?
faqs.answer: You'll experience picturesque natural landscapes characterized by lush green scenery, including dense forests of beeches and oaks. The Aralar mountains are recognized for their ecological value, offering a beautiful backdrop for your ride. The Alto de Beruete is particularly noted as a "very picturesque place."

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route runs in proximity to and occasionally intersects with other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Plazaola Greenway (Vía Verde del Plazaola), which offers additional scenic routes, some featuring tunnels. Other official trails along this route include the Asturian Way and the Sakanako Donejakue bidea.
